doc.title: Trading debt securities — mark to market through income
doc.event: Kite Co. holds TRADING debt securities with an amortized cost of $100,000 at year-end. Prepare the year-end fair value adjustment.

entries.caption: Year-end: adjust trading portfolio to fair value (cost 100,000 → FV 106,000)

lines.label: FV − amortized cost
lines.why: A valuation account rides beside the investment so amortized cost stays intact underneath — presentation shows fair value.
lines.trap: Adjust the ALLOWANCE, not Debt Investments itself — the amortization schedule keeps running on the untouched cost.

lines.account: Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Income
lines.label: straight to net income
lines.why: Trading securities exist to be flipped — holding gains are performance NOW, so they hit income even though nothing sold.
lines.trap: INCOME, not OCI. The classification (trading vs AFS) decides where the unrealized change lands — that's the entire chapter in one choice.

lines.account: Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Income
lines.label: the drop, in income now
lines.why: Same account, debit side — losses on trading positions hit earnings immediately.
lines.trap: Asset chair this time: fair value DOWN = loss (the mirror of Ch. 13's fair-value-option liability, where FV up was the loss).

lines.label: allowance goes negative
lines.why: The adjustment account can carry a credit balance, netting the presentation below cost.
lines.trap: Next year adjusts the ALLOWANCE to the new target — the entry is always 'move the adjustment to (FV − cost),' not the full swing from zero.

memorize.body: Year-end target: Fair Value Adjustment balance = FV − amortized cost. The ENTRY is whatever moves the existing balance to that target.
Trading = Turnover = Through income. Held to sell soon → the market's opinion IS this period's performance.
Interest revenue still accrues on trading debt exactly as amortized-cost math dictates — marking to market is an ADDITIONAL layer, not a replacement.

questions.prompt: Trading debt (amortized cost 100,000) has year-end FV 106,000. The 6,000 unrealized gain is reported…

distractors.feedback: 'Nothing sold' doesn't mean 'nothing reported' for TRADING securities — unrealized holding gains go straight to net income. (Zero-until-sold is the HTM treatment.)

accountBank.account: Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Income
Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Equity

build.scaffold: Two lines, always: the adjustment account moves to (FV − cost), and the other side names where the classification sends it — for trading, that's Income. The '—Equity' account in the bank belongs to the next scenario.
